title: biscutella apula var. lyrata
intro: Biscutella apula var. lyrata, also known as Alpine Pennycress, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its small, white or pale yellow flowers and narrow, pointed leaves. Traditionally, Alpine Pennycress has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting respiratory health. Alpine Pennycress is typically prepared as a tea or infused in oil for its medicinal uses, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
